pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh: script attempts to download BBBC008_v1_images.zip but fails to find target file due to filename mismatch (looking for 'BBBC008_v1_A01_s1_w1.TIF' which does not exist in archive), falls back to generating synthetic 512x512 uint16 image
Verified in task.json description and export_result.sh script which checks for these three exact files

pi_items.verified_value: Python skimage analysis: Gaussian background subtraction (sigma=50), Otsu thresholding, Watershed segmentation with distance transform and peak local maxima for marker generation, integrated density measurement via intensity_image.sum()
pi_items.source: Code inspection of setup_task.sh lines 69-128: ground truth calculation uses filters.gaussian, filters.threshold_otsu, morphology.watershed, feature.peak_local_max, and measure.regionprops
Verified in verifier.py line 76 which reads 'task_info.get("metadata", {}).get("tolerance_percent", 15)' and uses it for accuracy checking; also used in setup_task.sh ground truth calculation

pi_items.metadata_value: Implicit claim: uses BBBC008 real dataset (from setup_task.sh line 20 comment and URL)
pi_items.verified_value: BBBC008 download is attempted but fails due to filename mismatch: script looks for 'BBBC008_v1_A01_s1_w1.TIF' or '*w1.TIF' pattern, but actual BBBC008_v1_images.zip contains files named 'AS_09125_050116000001_*_slice*_channel*.tif'. Download succeeds (verified URL is accessible, 7.6MB archive), but file extraction fails, triggering fallback to synthetic data generation.
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed BBBC008 is real dataset of human HT29 colon cancer cells with Hoechst (DNA) staining. Downloaded and inspected BBBC008_v1_images.zip from https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC008/BBBC008_v1_images.zip. File listing shows 24 TIF files with naming pattern 'human_ht29_colon_cancer_2_images/AS_09125_050116000001_*_slice*_channel*.tif' - no files match the pattern the script seeks.

privileged_info_summary: The task intends to use BBBC008, a real public dataset of human HT29 colon cancer cells (Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection, accessible at https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC008/). However, due to a filename mismatch in the setup script, the download succeeds but file extraction fails, causing the task to generate synthetic data instead. The synthetic data consists of a 512x512 uint16 image with 30 simulated G1-phase cells (intensity ~20000) and 10 G2-phase cells (intensity ~40000), generated with random seed 42. The ground truth calculation uses Python skimage with Otsu thresholding and watershed segmentation. A 15% tolerance is applied for accuracy evaluation. The three expected outputs are measurements.csv, histogram.png, and g1_peak_report.txt.
